q1.prompt: A 42-year-old resident of Rhode Island presents with fever and hemolytic anemia; microscopy of a Giemsa-stained thin blood smear reveals tetrad arrangements of ring forms within the erythrocytes. Which of the following is the most likely causative agent?

q2.prompt: A 55-year-old man presents with fatigue and numbness in his lower extremities; laboratory investigations reveal megaloblastic anemia and a vitamin B12 deficiency. He reports a long-standing habit of consuming raw freshwater fish. Which organism is most likely responsible for this clinical presentation?

q3.prompt: A 48-year-old Cambodian man with a history of corticosteroid therapy for asthma develops refractory septic shock caused by Escherichia coli bacteremia. Bronchoscopy reveals numerous rhabditiform larvae in the endotracheal secretions. Which mechanism explains how the bacteria reached the bloodstream?

options.label: Direct penetration of the larvae through the skin
Entry of larvae through the cribriform plate
Ingestion of infectious eggs contaminating food
Larval transport of bacteria during internal autoinfection
Migration of adult worms into the biliary tract

q4.prompt: A patient is treated for an intestinal fluke infection with Praziquantel. Which of the following best describes the primary mechanism of action of this medication against the parasite?

options.label: Activation of chloride channels causing flaccid paralysis
Inhibition of microtubule polymerization by binding to β-tubulin
Inhibition of parasite haemoglobin digestion in the vacuole
Prevention of DNA synthesis by inhibiting reverse transcriptase
Promotion of calcium influx leading to spastic paralysis

q5.prompt: During a laboratory investigation of a stool specimen, a white, ribbon-like gravid proglottid is recovered. India ink injection reveals 22 primary lateral uterine branches on one side of the segment. Which parasite is identified?

q6.prompt: A 35-year-old traveler returning from Thailand presents with a three-day history of bloody, mucoid diarrhea and tenesmus; stool microscopy shows motile trophozoites containing ingested red blood cells. Which finding is most likely to be observed during a colonoscopy with biopsy?

options.label: Diffuse inflammatory foci throughout the mucosa
Multiple inflammatory nodules in the lamina propria
Presence of adult worms embedded in the mucosal layer
Squamous cell carcinoma of the intestinal wall

q7.prompt: A 50-year-old sheep farmer is found to have a 10-centimetre unilocular cyst in the right lobe of the liver with "eggshell calcification" on CT scan. During surgical resection, which complication is the surgeon most concerned about if the cyst ruptures?

q8.prompt: A 40-year-old man from Brazil presents with dilated cardiomyopathy and megacolon; physical examination reveals a laterally displaced point of maximal impact. Which of the following vectors is associated with the transmission of this disease?

q9.prompt: A patient presents with fever, upper abdominal pain, and marked eosinophilia; an investigation reveals the presence of a liver fluke infection associated with the frequent consumption of raw watercress. What is the infective stage of this parasite?

q10.prompt: A routine stool examination of an asymptomatic child reveals brownish, barrel-shaped eggs (60 x 45 µm) with characteristic clear mucoid plugs at both ends. Which parasite does this represent?
